reasoning: Critical correctness issue detected: floating-point precision in l_discount BETWEEN predicate causes wrong results (22593.35 difference). Must fix immediately before pursuing performance optimizations. Current performance (23.7ms) is competitive with DuckDB (20ms), so correctness takes absolute priority. After fixing, iteration 2 can apply SIMD vectorization and other optimizations.
focus_areas: Fix l_discount floating-point precision: use epsilon-tolerant comparison or convert to fixed-point integers (multiply by 100, compare as int32_t)
Preferred: fixed-point conversion (discount * 100 → int32_t) enables integer SIMD in future iterations
strategy_notes: CRITICAL: Apply the semantic/correctness fix (recommendation 0) exclusively in this iteration. The issue is that l_discount is compared as double (>= 0.05, <= 0.07) but TPC-H specifies DECIMAL(15,2) with exact arithmetic. Two viable approaches: (1) Epsilon-tolerant comparison: >= 0.05 - 1e-9, <= 0.07 + 1e-9 (quick fix), OR (2) Fixed-point conversion at load time: multiply discount by 100, compare as int32_t (5 <= discount_int <= 7). Prefer option 2 because it eliminates floating-point rounding entirely and enables integer SIMD vectorization in iteration 2. After validation passes, iteration 2 has 1 remaining budget to apply SIMD filtering (16.7ms → 4-6ms expected) and other performance optimizations to beat DuckDB significantly.
notes: Priority rule: Correctness fixes FIRST. Do not apply performance optimizations until validation passes. The fixed-point conversion (option 2) is slightly more complex but yields better long-term performance. Ensure thorough validation with all TPC-H discount boundary values after fix.
